CHANGE RECORD 
 
 
When the quality of the products changes, the QuID is updated and a row is added to this table. 
The third column specifies which sections or sub -sections have been updated. The fourth  
column should mention the version of the product to which the change applies.

I INTRODUCTION  
 
 
 
I.1 Product name 
 
OMI_CLIMATE_SST_NORTHWESTSHELF_ area_averaged_anomalies 
OMI_CLIMATE_SST_NORTHWESTSHELF_ trend 
 
 
I.2 Indicator evaluation method 
 
The European Northwest Shelf Sea Surface Temperature (SST) Ocean Monitoring Indicators  
(OMIs) are derived from the CMEMS Reprocessed European North West Shelf Iberia -Biscay- 
Irish areas (REP ATL) SST L4 product1. The REP ATL L4 product provides the SSTs that are used to 
compute all the IBI SST OMIs, i.e. the time series of SST anomalies 
(nws_omi_tempsal_sst_area_averaged_anomalies) and the 2D map of SST trend 
(nws_omi_tempsal_sst_trend). Anomalies are computed against the 1991-2020 reference 
period. In detail, daily maps of REP ATL L4 data from 1982 were averaged to obtain SST monthly 
maps, which were then used to build the time series of monthly mean SST values averaged over 
the entire basin. 
Trend analysis has been performed by using the X -11 seasonal adjustment procedure (Pezzulli 
et al. 2005). This procedure decomposes the input SST time series into the sum of three distinct 
components: 
SSTt = Tt + St + It, 
where Tt is the trend component, St and It the seasonal and the irregular components. The X - 
11 procedure has the effect of filtering the input time series with Tt acting as a low  bandpass 
filter for interannual variations. The only difference with respect to the standard X-11 procedure 
is that we substituted the 13 -term moving-average window (used to estimate Tt)  with a 25 -
term moving-average window, i.e. from 1-year to 2-year low-pass filter. This makes more robust 
the estimation of the trend component Tt.
